rubric.evidence: Standalone runnability probe (transformers.js 3.7.5, audio-classification, dtype q8, onnx-community/Musical-Instrument-Classification-ONNX) run in headless Chrome via a Web Worker on a real CC BY-SA violin recording (Wikimedia 'Violin open string.ogg', decoded to 16 kHz mono) returned a full 9-class distribution with the CORRECT top label: Violin 0.303, Acoustic_Guitar 0.149, Electro_Guitar 0.120, flute 0.116, Trumpet 0.099, Hi_Hats 0.065, Keyboard 0.055, Bass_Guitar 0.052, Drum_set 0.041 — real probabilities that sum to ~1 (a genuine classification head, not head-less/undefined logits). The page decodes and runs the pipeline in a Web Worker (models/instrument-classification/worker.js).
rubric.notes: A second probe (a flute clip) top-labelled Trumpet 0.33 with flute at 0.08 — a real out-of-distribution miss, disclosed in the page's honesty caveat. The head is genuine and correct on the in-distribution violin; imperfect timbre discrimination is a property of this compact model, surfaced (not hidden) via the full distribution.

rubric.evidence: audio-classification output is ranked instrument labels with probabilities. The page renders the full 9-class distribution as confidence bars (not just the winner), a big top-instrument readout, and a raw probability table — matching the per-task template 'produces ranked audio class labels with probabilities'. Probe output above confirms shape + semantic sanity (top=Violin on a violin clip).

At-a-glance declares the exact model id (linked), task (audio-classification), architecture (Wav2Vec2ForSequenceClassification), the 9 classes, dtype (q8, onnx/model_quantized.onnx), and backend (WASM; WebGPU when an adapter exists). Inference runs off the main thread in models/instrument-classification/worker.js via the shared lib/webai.js loadPipeline.

Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (transformers.js owns transformers-cache; lib/model-cache.js scans it). First-visit download-required + Download button is the shared code path proven end-to-end on the sibling speech-emotion-recognition / silero-vad builds (reload → state=ready, no Download button).
rubric.notes: auto-init-on-cached is the shared loader path; the full 95 MB reload verdict is manual-evidenced, pending an agent read.

rubric.evidence: Loader announces checking → downloading (progress) → ready via role=status aria-live; errors surface on the page (.status.err) with a Retry path from the shared loader; the WASM path needs no WebGPU. An explicit honesty caveat about the compact model's fallibility is shown in At-a-glance (read the distribution, not just the top label).

Controls exercised across the ladder: sample chips (aria-pressed), file dropzone (role=button, Enter/Space), mic record, Identify-instrument button; practical adds a multi-file queue + bucket board; wild adds window/hop range sliders with live <output>; multi-model runs two models with two loaders. Keyboard/focus verdict on both classes pending an agent matrix read.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable parts pass; keyboard/tap verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: Every confidence bar, big readout, timeline cell, bucket, and probability row is derived from the real pipeline output — no canned distribution. The honesty caveat frames the top label as a signal, not a verdict.

Buttons have accessible names; instrument icons are aria-hidden with a text label alongside; status is a live region; range sliders (wild) are label-associated with a visible <output>. Conformance images-have-alt / buttons-have-names pass. Full SR pass on both classes pending an agent read.

Layout reuses the proven speech-emotion patterns: confidence bars on a minmax track with min-inline-size:0; the practical bucket board and the multi-model two-column view are repeat(auto-fit,minmax(min(100%,240px),1fr)) grids that collapse to one column on mobile (css-layout guidance). Automated responsive matrix (no-horizontal-overflow at 1280×800 and 360×740) pending a clean headless run in this environment.
rubric.notes: auto-checkable overflow/clipping verdict is pending the responsive-check harness; legibility/tap verdict is manual-evidenced.

rubric.evidence: Inference is off the main thread (Web Worker); the instrument-over-time (wild) page classifies windows sequentially in the worker and streams per-window results back, so the UI stays responsive (identify-inp-causes guidance). No blocking main-thread inference loop.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use auto-fit/minmax grids and flex-wrap with min-inline-size:0 and logical properties rather than fixed widths so the confidence bars, bucket board, and two-column multi-model layout never overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — confidence bars use a minmax track with min-inline-size:0 on the bar; the practical bucket board and multi-model comparison are repeat(auto-fit,minmax(min(100%,240px),1fr)) grids that collapse to one column on mobile; all sizing uses logical properties (inline-size/block-size).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared with the built speech-emotion-recognition demo whose responsive matrix records overflow 0 at 360px and 1280px

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep interactions responsive by keeping heavy JavaScript off the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the wav2vec2 pipeline runs in a Web Worker; the instrument-over-time page classifies windows in the worker and streams results, so the main thread only draws the waveform/timeline.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state in a live region; keep icons decorative and expose a text label; associate sliders with visible values.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — status is role=status aria-live; instrument icons are aria-hidden beside a text instrument label; the window/hop sliders (wild) are label-associated with a live <output>.

openQuestions: Does the ~95 MB q8 model download + init stay within acceptable memory/time on a mid-range mobile device?
Should low top-1 confidence (e.g. < 0.35) be surfaced as an explicit 'uncertain' state to temper the headline label on out-of-distribution timbres?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for instrument-classification: run the responsive-check harness for a clean overflow/console/network verdict, read the mobile+desktop screenshots, confirm bars + bucket board + two-column multi-model + tap targets + focus, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Consider adding a confidence-gated 'uncertain' badge when top-1 < 0.35 to make the honesty caveat concrete in the UI.

summary: Revision-1 critique for instrument-classification (audio-classification, wav2vec2, q8). Real in-browser inference is confirmed by a headless-Chrome worker probe: a CC BY-SA violin clip returned a full 9-class distribution with the correct top label (Violin 0.30). The head is genuine; a flute clip's Trumpet miss is a disclosed model limitation surfaced via the full distribution. Auto-checkable dimensions pass; the full-page responsive matrix + tap/focus verdicts await a clean agent run.
